title: Color Palette Generator
description: Generate complementary, analogous, triadic harmonies from any base color. Copy any color as HEX, RGB, or HSL.

results.empty: Enter a hex color to generate a palette

howToUse.label: Enter a base color
howToUse.description: Type a HEX color code (e.g. #3b82f6) or click the color picker.

howToUse.label: Explore harmonies
howToUse.description: The tool generates complementary, analogous, triadic, and split-complementary color schemes automatically.

howToUse.label: Copy any color
howToUse.description: Click any swatch to copy it as HEX, RGB, or HSL.

faq.question: What is color harmony?
faq.answer: Color harmony refers to combinations of colors that are visually pleasing. Common harmonies are based on the color wheel: complementary (opposite), analogous (adjacent), triadic (equally spaced), and split-complementary (one color + two adjacent to its complement).

faq.question: What is the difference between tints and shades?
faq.answer: A tint is a color mixed with white (lighter). A shade is a color mixed with black (darker). Both preserve the hue and saturation while changing the lightness.

faq.question: What are complementary colors?
faq.answer: Complementary colors sit opposite each other on the color wheel (hue difference of 180°). They create high contrast and vibrant pairings. For example, blue and orange, or red and green are complementary.

faq.question: What are analogous colors?
faq.answer: Analogous colors are adjacent on the color wheel (hue difference of ±30°). They create harmonious, low-contrast palettes that feel natural — like the colors of a sunset.
